to  specific  checks,  they  are  added  to  the  name  of
the condition, such as Attack Impaired, Fighting Im-
paired, Perception Impaired, and so forth

act_0038.subject: Your Strength
act_0038.raw:
Your Strength rank applies to:

•

Damage dealt by your unarmed and strength-based
attacks

act_0039.subject: Your Stamina
act_0039.raw:
Your Stamina
modifier applies to:

•
•

•

Toughness defense, for resisting damage

act_0040.subject: Your Agility
act_0040.raw:
Your Agility rank applies to:

•

•
•
•

Dodge defense, for avoiding ranged attacks and oth-
er hazards

act_0041.subject: Your Dexterity
act_0041.raw:
Your Dexterity rank applies to:

•
•
•

Attack checks for ranged attacks

act_0042.subject: FIGHTING
act_0042.raw:
FIGHTING (FGT)

Fighting  measures  your  character’s  ability  in  close  com-
bat, from hitting a target to ducking and weaving around
any counter-attacks

act_0043.subject: Your Fighting
act_0043.raw:
Your Fighting rank applies to:

•
•

Attack checks for close attacks

act_0044.subject: Your Intellect
act_0044.raw:
Your Intellect modifier applies to:

•

•

Expertise,  Investigation, Technology,  and Treatment
skill checks

act_0045.subject: Your Awareness
act_0045.raw:
Your Awareness
modifier applies to:

•  Will defense, for resisting attacks on your mind

act_0046.subject: Your Presence
act_0046.raw:
Your Presence modifier applies to:

•
•

Deception, Intimidation, and Persuasion skill checks

act_0047.subject: Dexterity
act_0047.raw:
Dexterity: A creature with no Dexterity cannot manip-
ulate objects and hence cannot make physical attacks

act_0048.subject: Fighting
act_0048.raw:
Fighting: A creature with no Fighting is incapable of
making any sort of close attack (but may still be able
to launch ranged attacks, if it has Dexterity)

act_0049.subject: Animate
act_0049.raw:
Animate, but nonliving, constructs such as ro-
bots or zombies have Strength, Agility, and Dexterity, and
may  have  ranks  of  Awareness  and  Presence  (if  aware  of
their environment or capable of

act_0050.subject: Ability
act_0050.raw:
Ability • Trained Only • Interaction • Manipulation • Requires Tools

The skill name line and the line below it contain the fol-
lowing information:

Skill Name: What the skill is called

act_0051.subject: Requires Tools
act_0051.raw:
Requires Tools: If “Requires Tools” is included on the line
below the skill’s name, you need to have the proper tools
to use the skill

act_0052.subject: Climb
act_0052.raw:
A ladder

A knotted rope

A rope

An uneven surface, like a rock-face

A rough surface, like a brick wall

An air duct, chimney, or other area
where you can brace against two
opposite walls

A corner

act_0053.subject: Uses
act_0053.raw:
Uses of Deception in action rounds are generally standard
actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move
action by taking a –5 penalty to your check

act_0054.subject: Art
act_0054.raw:
Art, Business, Carpentry, Cooking, Criminal, Current Events, Dance, History, Journalism, Law, Law Enforcement, Medicine, Mili-
tary, Music, Magic, Philosophy, Politics, Popular Culture, Psychiatry, Sc

act_0055.subject: Example
act_0055.raw:
Example: Figuring out a particular clue involving a government conspiracy requires an Investigation or Expertise: Poli-
tics check

act_0056.subject: Uses
act_0056.raw:
Uses of Intimidation in ac-
tion rounds are generally standard actions, although you
can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5
penalty to your check

act_0057.subject: Discerning
act_0057.raw:
Discerning
details—such  as  clearly  hearing  conversation  or  reading
fine  text—requires  at  least  three  degrees  of  success  on
the Perception check

act_0058.subject: Intellect
act_0058.raw:
Intellect • Trained Only • Manipulation • Requires Tools

Technology  covers  operating,  building,  repairing,  and
generally working with technological devices and equip-
ment

act_0059.subject: Disarming
act_0059.raw: Disarming an explosive also requires a Technology check

act_0060.subject: Intellect
act_0060.raw:
Intellect • Trained Only • Manipulation • Requires Tools

STABILIZE

You’re trained in treating injuries and ailments

act_0061.raw:
Grabbing Finesse

Improved Aim

act_0062.subject: DIEHARD GENERAL When
act_0062.raw:
DIEHARD

GENERAL

When  your  condition  becomes  dying  (see  Conditions
in  the  Action  &  Adventure  chapter)  you  automatically
stabilize  on  the  following  round  without  any  need  for  a
F

act_0063.subject: Multiple
act_0063.raw:
Multiple uses of
Inspire do not stack, only the highest bonus applies

act_0064.subject: SET
act_0064.raw:
SET-UP

COMBAT, RANKED

speed in the round, regardless of the number of attacks
you  make

act_0065.subject: Targets
act_0065.raw:
Targets  resist  using  De-
ception, Insight, or Will defense

act_0066.subject: Targets
act_0066.raw: Targets resist with Insight, Intimidation, or Will defense

act_0067.subject: Perception
act_0067.raw:
It requires a Perception check with a DC
equal to your Awareness check result to determine you’re
not dead because your bodily functions are so slow

act_0068.subject: Flight
act_0068.raw:
So, for example, the ac-
tion of the Flight effect is “free” and activating it grants the
character a Flight speed rank equal to the effect rank

act_0069.subject: Standard
act_0069.raw:
•

Standard: Using the effect requires a standard action

act_0070.subject: Move
act_0070.raw: •  Move: Using the effect requires a move action

act_0071.subject: Touching
act_0071.raw:
Touching an unwilling subject requires an un-
armed attack check against the subject’s Parry

act_0072.subject: Extra Condition
act_0072.raw:
Extra  Condition:  Your  Affliction  imposes  an  additional
condition per degree of success

act_0073.subject: Then
act_0073.raw:
Then choose the action required to assume
your Alternate Form: if it requires a move action, subtract 1 power point from the total cost of the effects

act_0074.subject: Thus
act_0074.raw:
Thus, foes with Visual Conceal-
ment (the most common accurate sense) can be quite effective against characters relying on perception range attacks,
unless the attacker has an unusual accurate sense t

act_0075.subject: Area
act_0075.raw:
Area: Concealment with Affects Others (previously) or At-
tack (immediately following) may have this extra, affecting
everything  in  the  area

act_0076.subject: Attacks
act_0076.raw:
Attacks hitting the covering ob-
ject  damage  it  normally  (see  Damaging  Objects,  page
244)

act_0077.subject: Indirect
act_0077.raw:
Indirect effects can bypass the cover a created object
provides just like any other cover (see the Indirect modi-
fier)

act_0078.subject: Imposing
act_0078.raw:
Imposing
conditions on the target other than just trapping them
requires a separate effect, such as Affliction (see Afflic-
tion in this chapter), which you may wish to acquire as
an Alternate Effect

act_0079.subject: Strength
act_0079.raw:
Strength provides a “built-in” Damage effect: the ability
to hit things

act_0080.subject: Objects
act_0080.raw:
Objects  (targets  lacking  a  Stamina  rank)  take  damage
similar to other targets

act_0081.subject: Inanimate
act_0081.raw:
Inanimate objects are defenseless by definition and there-
fore subject to finishing attacks (see Finishing Attack in
the  Action  &  Adventure  chapter):  essentially,  you  can
choose  between  maki

act_0083.subject: DEFLECT DEFENSE Action
act_0083.raw:
DEFLECT

DEFENSE

Action: Standard • Range: Ranged
Duration: Instant • Cost: 1 point per rank

You can actively defend for characters other than yourself,
deflecting or diverting attacks against them

act_0084.subject: Like
act_0084.raw:
Like the defend action, Deflect does not work against area
effects  or  perception  ranged  attacks,  nor  versus  attacks
targeting defenses other than Dodge or Parry

act_0085.subject: EXTRAS Action
act_0085.raw:
EXTRAS

Action: Because it requires the defend action, Deflect cannot
take less than a standard action

act_0086.subject: Enhanced Dodge
act_0086.raw:
To create a kind of “deflec-
tion field” or similar effect that automatically deflects attacks
over a wide area, use an Enhanced Dodge and/or Enhanced
Parry effect with modifiers like Area and Selecti

act_0087.subject: Aura
act_0087.raw:
If your Aura damages
some targets but not others, apply the Selective or Lim-
ited modifiers (depending on whether or not the selec-
tivity is under your control)

act_0088.subject: Further
act_0088.raw:
Further  attacks  can “top  off”  your  Enhanced
Trait, restoring it to its full rank again, before it is com-
pletely faded

act_0089.subject: Example
act_0089.raw:
Example:  Rebound  has  rank  11  Energy  Absorption
that applies the energy of kinetic attacks to Strength

act_0090.subject: However
act_0090.raw:
However, if
Rebound is hit again on the following turn for Dam-
age  4,  he  gains  up  to  4  ranks  of  Strength,  up  to  his
maximum again

act_0091.subject: Absorption
act_0091.raw:
Absorption often includes the additional ef-
fect of Protection (Impervious and often Limited to the
absorbed type of attack) or even Immunity to the ab-
sorbed  attack

act_0092.subject: HOOD
act_0092.raw:
If you apply all of your limbs to a grab attempt (rather than
taking the option to leave some of them free), you gain a

HOOD: EXTRA LIMBS, NOT EXTRA ACTIONS

As a default, Extra Limbs do not grant ch

act_0093.subject: Extra Limbs
act_0093.raw:
As  an  option  for  including  some  combat  benefits  with
Extra  Limbs,  consider  allowing  the  application  of  the
Multiattack extra to the Strength of a character with Extra
Limbs, reflecting

act_0094.subject: Affects Corporeal
act_0094.raw:
Affects
Corporeal,  Area,  Penetrating,  etc.)  should  apply  to  the
Strength  of  a  character  with  Extra  Limbs  rather  than  to
the  Extra  Limbs  effect  itself

act_0095.subject: Affects Objects
act_0095.raw:
Affects Objects: Your Healing can also “heal” damage to
non-living  subjects

act_0096.subject: Area
act_0096.raw:
Area: Healing with this extra grants the same benefit to all
subjects in the affected area

act_0097.subject: Ranged
act_0097.raw:
Ranged:  Ranged  Healing  requires  an  attack  check  to
“touch” the subject with the Healing effect

act_0098.subject: Limited
act_0098.raw:
Limited: Examples of ways in which Healing may be Lim-
ited include: One Type of Damage (such as energy or blud-
geoning  damage),  Objects  (in  conjunction  with  Affects
Objects), Others (you can’t

act_0099.subject: So Illusions Resistible
act_0099.raw:
So Illusions
Resistible by Fortitude or Will have no effect on non-living
targets, for example

act_0100.subject: Affliction
act_0100.raw:
5 ranks: alteration effects, sensory Affliction effects,
emotion  effects,  entrapment  (grabbing,  snares,  or
bonds), fatigue effects, interaction skills, or a particu-
lar  Damage  effect,  descrip

act_0101.subject: Ranged
act_0101.raw:
Ranged: Affects Others Immunity may have this extra, al-
lowing it to grant its benefits at range

act_0102.subject: Sensory
act_0102.raw:
Sensory effects
(other than tactile) and those targeting Will still work on
you,  as  do  effects  with  the  Affects  Insubstantial  modi-
fier

act_0103.subject: Energy
act_0103.raw:
Energy
attacks  (other  than  the  energy  making  up  your  form,  to
which you have Immunity) damage you normally

act_0104.subject: Attack
act_0104.raw:
Attack: Applied to Insubstantial, this extra makes it into
a close range effect able to turn targets Insubstantial

act_0105.subject: Rank
act_0105.raw:
Rank  1  requires  a  Perception
check (DC 20) to detect that you are Insubstantial, while 2
ranks mean you look entirely normal in Insubstantial form
(which may cause opponents to waste effort on you

act_0106.subject: FLAWS Action
act_0106.raw:
FLAWS

Action: If the action required for Luck Control is increased
beyond a reaction, it is only usable during your turn each

Effect:  Perception  Ranged  Damage,  Resisted  by  Will  •
4 points per

act_0107.subject: Ranged
act_0107.raw: Ranged: Luck Control normally requires no attack check

act_0108.subject: Resistible
act_0108.raw:
Resistible: Targets  of  your  Luck  Control  get  a  resistance
check—usually Dodge or Will—to avoid its effects

act_0109.subject: Targets
act_0109.raw:
Targets  failing  a Will  resistance
check against your effect DC first become dazed, then
compelled, as they try to fight off your influence

act_0110.subject: FLAWS Close
act_0110.raw:
FLAWS

Close: Applied to Ranged Mind Reading, Close Mind Read-
ing requires a close attack check to touch an unwilling tar-
get and physical contact throughout the effect’s duration

act_0111.subject: Ranged
act_0111.raw:
Ranged: Ranged Mind Reading requires a ranged attack
check in addition to the effect’s normal resistance check

act_0112.subject: EXTRAS Attack
act_0112.raw:
EXTRAS

Attack:  A  Morph  Attack  imposes  a  different  appearance
on  the  target  creature

act_0113.subject: Unlike
act_0113.raw:
Unlike  an  Affliction  that  imposes
the transformed condition, a Morph Attack is entirely cos-
metic: you can’t change the target’s traits other than ap-
pearance

act_0114.subject: Metamorph
act_0114.raw:
If you have
the Metamorph extra, then targets that resist your effect
treat you as if you had your normal traits, and not those
granted by your Metamorph form

act_0115.subject: Objects
act_0115.raw:
Objects
thrown into targets as attacks base their damage off your
power rank as if it were your Strength rank

act_0116.subject: The GM
act_0116.raw:
The
GM may allow Continuous Move Object as a variation that
is not disrupted when you are unable to maintain it, but
that still requires your conscious attention to do anything
other than have the aff

act_0117.subject: Permeate

act_0118.subject: Wall
act_0118.raw:
If you have the Wall-crawling power (later in Movement),
any surface you can climb provides you with a handhold

act_0119.subject: Reaching
act_0119.raw:
Reaching alternate timelines or parallel worlds
requires at least 2 ranks of Dimension-Travel

act_0120.subject: EXTRAS Affects Insubstantial
act_0120.raw:
EXTRAS

Affects Insubstantial: Nullify does not require this modi-
fier to affect insubstantial targets, or the Insubstantial ef-
fect itself

act_0121.subject: Area
act_0121.raw:
Area:  An  Area  Nullify  effect  works  on  all  targets  in  the
area

act_0122.subject: Selective
act_0122.raw:
Selective: If you have an Area Nullify effect, this extra al-
lows you to choose who in the area is affected, nullifying
some targets and not others

act_0123.subject: Limited
act_0123.raw:
Limited to One Task: Your Quickness applies to only one
particular  task,  such  as  reading,  mathematical  calcula-
tions, and so forth

act_0124.subject: See Super
act_0124.raw:
See  Super-Speed  for  some  examples  of  the  su-
per-fast attacks speedsters might have, in addition to
their Quickness

act_0125.subject: Another
act_0125.raw:
Another  effect
might potentially have the flaw Remote-Sensing Depen-
dent, such as an Affliction that targets only remote view-
ers observing a target or an area as a means of blocking or
deterring t

act_0126.subject: Like
act_0126.raw:
Like  all  sensory  effects,  Senses  uses  the  sense  types  de-
scribed on page 178 as descriptors

act_0127.subject: Area
act_0127.raw:
Area: The Area modifier only applies to Senses that Af-
fect Others, and only to extend their benefits to every-

Limited:  Some  Senses  may  be  Limited  to  only  sensing
certain  things  or  only

act_0128.subject: Active
act_0128.raw:
Do not apply the
Active modifier to Heroic minions, as this modifier already
includes it

act_0129.subject: Targets
act_0129.raw:
Targets  failing  the  Fortitude  resistance  check  against
your effect DC become fatigued, then exhausted, and
finally asleep as they succumb

act_0130.subject: Targets
act_0130.raw:
Targets  fail-
ing  the  Fortitude  resistance  check  against  your  effect
DC become dazed, stunned, and finally incapacitated,
passing out from the lack of oxygen

act_0131.subject: Heroes
act_0131.raw:
Heroes with Super-Speed often have additional powers
based on their speed, particularly things like Air Control
(whipping  up  powerful  winds,  see  Element  Control,
previously) or modifiers to thei

act_0132.subject: What
act_0132.raw:
What you can transform affects cost per rank:

•

2 points: Transform one thing or substance into one
other thing or substance, such as metal into wood,
iron into glass, or broken objects into repaire

act_0133.subject: Transforming
act_0133.raw:
Transforming living or otherwise animate beings as op-
posed to inanimate matter requires an effect other than
Transform

act_0134.subject: Transforming
act_0134.raw:
Transforming  someone’s  devices  or  equipment  requires
targeting  them  first:  characters  can  make  Dodge  resis-
tance  checks  for  their  held  or  worn  items,  with  a  +5  cir-
cumstance b

act_0135.subject: Targeting
act_0135.raw:
Targeting  a  worn  suit  of  armor  requires  an  attack  check
and allows the wearer a Dodge resistance check (with no
modifier for a large item)

act_0136.subject: Perception
act_0136.raw:
Perception: Applied to a Ranged Affects Others Variable,
this extra allows you to grant the benefits of the effect to
any target you can accurately perceive

act_0137.subject: Ranged
act_0137.raw:
Ranged:  A Variable  effect  with  Affects  Others  may  have
the Ranged extra to improve the range at which you can
grant the effect to another

act_0138.subject: Any
act_0138.raw:
Any further uses of Weaken on the
subject have no effect until the ability recovers to a rank
of at least –5

act_0139.subject: Weaken
act_0139.raw:
Weaken with Affects Objects and the right descriptor(s) can
lower the traits provided by a device (see the Removable
flaw in this chapter and the Gadgets & Gear chapter)

act_0140.subject: Progressive
act_0140.raw:
Progressive:  A  Progressive  Weaken  effect  reduces  the
affected  traits  each  round  until  the  target  successfully
resists

act_0141.subject: Combined
act_0141.raw:
Combined with Precise
(previously), you can use an Area Weaken to selectively af-
fect only certain traits of certain targets

act_0142.subject: Imperious
act_0142.raw:
the first
is the Imperious extra (a +1 point per rank modifier),
and the second is the flaw Limited to Blunt Physical
Attacks  (a  –3  points  per  rank  modifier)  for  a  total
modifier of –2

act_0143.subject: Since Protection
act_0143.raw:
Since Protection costs 1 point per rank,
the  –2  modifier  increases  the  number  of  ranks  per
power point, so the final cost is 1 power point per 3
ranks of Impervious Protection Limited to Blunt

act_0144.subject: Generally
act_0144.raw:
Generally, this extra applies to effects like Heal or Weaken,
allowing them to work on objects in the same way as they
do  living  creatures

act_0145.subject: Affects Insubstantial
act_0145.raw:
Affects Insubstantial

1-2 flat points

Effect works on insubstantial beings at half (1 rank) or full (2 ranks) effect

act_0146.subject: Affects Objects
act_0146.raw:
Affects Objects

+0-1 per rank

Fortitude resisted effect works on objects

act_0147.subject: Affects Others
act_0147.raw:
Affects Others

+0-1 per rank

Personal effect works on others

act_0148.subject: Alternate Resistance
act_0148.raw:
Alternate Resistance

+0-1 per rank

Effect uses a different resistance

act_0149.subject: Dimensional
act_0149.raw:
Dimensional

1-3 flat points

Effect works on targets in other dimensions

act_0150.subject: Selective Sleep Split Subtle Sustained
act_0150.raw:
Selective

Sleep

Split

Subtle

Sustained

Triggered

+1 per rank

+0 per rank

1 flat per rank

1-2 flat points

+0 per rank

1 flat per rank

Resistible effect works only on the targets you choose

act_0151.subject: Before
act_0151.raw:
Before giving a character Alternate Effects, it is wise to ask, “Is an array really needed for this concept?” Some concepts,
such as a variety of different attacks, clearly call for an array

act_0152.subject: Making
act_0152.raw:
Making the base effect of an array
Dynamic requires 1 power point

act_0153.subject: Potential
act_0153.raw:
Potential  targets  in  the  area  are  permitted
a  Dodge  resistance  check  (DC  10  +  effect  rank)  to  avoid
some of the effect (reflecting ducking for cover, dodging

190

act_0154.subject: Targets
act_0154.raw:
Targets get
a Dodge resistance check, as usual, but if the check is
successful suffer no effect (rather than half)

act_0155.subject: Percep
act_0155.raw:
Percep-
tion area effects neither require an attack check nor
allow a Dodge resistance check, although targets still
get a normal resistance check against the effect

act_0156.subject: Targets
act_0156.raw:
Targets behind a
solid barrier (such as on the other side of that glass
wall or invisible shield) are unaffected, but the unseen
or concealed target is, even though Fear-Master can’t
perceive him, sin

act_0157.subject: ATTACK
act_0157.raw:
ATTACK

+0 COST PER RANK

This  extra  applies  to  personal  range  effects,  making
them into attack effects

act_0158.subject: HOMING FLAT
act_0158.raw:
HOMING

FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK

This modifier grants a ranged effect an additional oppor-
tunity to hit

act_0159.subject: Increasing
act_0159.raw:
Increasing the range of an effect
from personal to close requires either the Affects Others
or Attack extras (see their descriptions)

act_0160.subject: LINKED FLAT
act_0160.raw:
LINKED

FLAT • 0 POINTS

This modifier applies to two or more effects, linking them
together so they only work in conjunction as one

act_0161.subject: Since Damage
act_0161.raw:
Since Damage
requires a Toughness check and Affliction requires a
Dodge check, the target checks against them sepa-
rately, making a Toughness resistance check against
the damage of the lightning and

act_0162.subject: Cap
act_0162.raw:
(To  do  these
things,  Cap  might  take  the  stand-alone  effects  as
Alternate Effects.)

+1 COST PER RANK

A Multiattack effect allows you to hit multiple targets, or a
single target multiple time

act_0163.subject: Example
act_0163.raw:
Example:  The  Protection  effect  is  permanent,
meaning  it  always  protects  the  character,  but
concentrating or trying harder does not make the
effect more protective, nor can the character use

act_0164.subject: Stonewall
act_0164.raw:
Stonewall’s  player
applies  the  Activation  flaw  to  the  power,  saying
Stonewall has to concentrate and take a standard
action  to  assume  his  rock-form

act_0165.subject: Concentration
act_0165.raw:
Concentration

–1 per rank

Sustained effect becomes concentration duration

act_0166.subject: COST PER RANK Using
act_0166.raw:
-1 COST PER RANK

Using  a  Distracting  effect  requires  more  concentration
than usual, causing you become vulnerable when you use
the effect, until the start of your next turn

act_0167.subject: GRAB
act_0167.raw:
GRAB-BASED

-1 COST PER RANK

An attack effect with this flaw requires you to successfully
grab a target before using the effect (see Grab, page 248)

act_0168.subject: PERMANENT
act_0168.raw:
PERMANENT

-1 COST PER RANK

A continuous effect with this flaw becomes permanent in
duration

act_0169.subject: REMOVABLE FLAT
act_0169.raw:
REMOVABLE

FLAT • -1 OR -2 POINTS PER 5 POINTS

Removable applies to the power as a whole and not in-
dividual effects, although it may apply to a power with
only  one  effect

act_0170.subject: Example
act_0170.raw:
Example:  Ultramarine’s  armor  provides  Veronica
with  a  number  of  effects,  including  Damage,  En-
hanced  Strength,  Flight,  Protection,  and  Senses

act_0171.subject: Thus
act_0171.raw:
Thus the Equipment advantage provides 5 points worth of equipment per rank
(or 1 power point)

act_0172.subject: Typical
act_0172.raw:
Typical side effects include Affliction,
Damage, or Weaken, or the base effect itself (it essentially
rebounds  and  affects  you  instead)

act_0173.subject: The Side Effect
act_0173.raw:
The  Side  Effect  does
not require an attack check and only affects you, although
the GM may permit some Side Effects with the Area modi-
fier on a case-by-case basis

act_0174.subject: The GM
act_0174.raw:
The GM may also allow a Complication Side Effect, which
essentially imposes a complication on you without award-
ing a hero point

act_0175.subject: Alternately
act_0175.raw:
Alternately,  instead  of  having  a  reliability  roll,  you  can
choose to have five uses where your effect works normal-
ly, then it stops working altogether until you can “recover”
it in some way

act_0176.subject: Situations
act_0176.raw:
Situations
where creative uses of descriptors have a significant game effect can be handled as power stunts: pick the effect that
best suits the desired outcome and treat it as an Alternate Effect of

act_0177.subject: Attacks
act_0177.raw:
Attacks:  Battlesuits  are  typically  equipped  with
some kind of weapon or weapons, based around var-
ious attack effects, particularly Damage

act_0178.subject: Weapons
act_0178.raw:
Weapons often have several different attacks as Alternate
Effects

act_0179.subject: Equipment
act_0179.raw:
Equipment  often  provides  the  Features
effect,  including  some  specific  equipment  Features  de-
scribed in this chapter

act_0180.subject: Repairing
act_0180.raw: Repairing  an  item  requires  a Technology  check

act_0181.subject: Investigation
act_0181.raw:
It
requires an Investigation or Perception check against the
results of the Sleight of Hand check used to conceal the
microphone to find it

act_0182.subject: Sleight
act_0182.raw:
It  requires  a  successful  attack  check  to  plant
a tracer on a target (or a Sleight of Hand check to plant
it  outside  of  action  time)

act_0183.subject: Night
act_0183.raw:
Night vi-

Camo  Clothing:  Clothing  in  the  right  camouflage  pat-
tern  for  the  environment  grants  a  +5  equipment  bonus
on  Stealth  checks  in  that  environment

act_0184.raw:
SCUBA (Self-Contained Underwater
Breathing Apparatus) gear provides two hours of oxygen,
and  characters  using  it  do  not  need  to  make  checks  for
suffocation unless the gear is damaged or disa

away (Reach 2) and provides the benefits of the Improved

act_0186.subject: Reach

away (Reach 3)

act_0187.raw:

with Accurate 1 due to the spread, but Limited to Dam-
age 3 against targets with Protection

act_0188.subject: Armor
act_0188.raw: Armor provides a Protection effect, a bonus to Toughness

act_0189.subject: Armor
act_0189.raw:
Armor has the following traits:

Category:  Armors  are  categorized  as  archaic  (ancient
styles of armor like chain- and plate-mail), modern (typi-
cally bulletproof composites and synthetics), and

act_0190.subject: Bombers
act_0190.raw:
Bombers may have machine guns and missiles, but also
have powerful bombs (Burst Area Damage 12 or higher)
they can drop on targets

act_0191.raw:

The  structure  of  the  headquarters  “heals”  any  damage
done to it over time

act_0194.subject: Fatigued
act_0194.raw:
Fatigued  characters  who  fail  a  check  become
exhausted, then incapacitated, at which point the charac-
ter’s condition becomes dying after another failed Forti-
tude check

act_0195.subject: Failure
act_0195.raw:
Failure  on  the
Fortitude  check  means  the  character  becomes  incapaci-
tated

act_0196.subject: Catching
act_0196.raw:
Catching a falling person or object requires a Dexterity
check  (DC  5)

act_0197.subject: Strength
act_0197.raw:
Strength provides a “built-in” Damage effect: the ability
to hit things

act_0198.subject: Inanimate
act_0198.raw:
Inanimate objects are defenseless by definition and there-
fore subject to finishing attacks (see the Finishing Attack
maneuver): essentially, you can choose between making
your attack on the object a

act_0199.subject: Most
act_0199.raw:
Most attacks target your active defenses,
Dodge and Parry: close attacks target Parry while ranged
attacks target Dodge

act_0200.subject: Defense Class
act_0200.raw:
Defense Class = defense + 10

checks against defenseless targets as routine checks (see

is guaranteed with an attack bonus of 0 or

act_0202.raw:
Dropping  to  a  prone  position  is  a  free  action,  although

His  unarmed  damage  (Strength)
rank is only 2, so he uses his speed rank of 10 for the
damage

Fortu-
nately,  Slingshot’s  helmet  provides  him  with  an

the  hero  manages  to  avoid  the  damage,  hoping

act_0205.raw:
Surprise
attacks occur during the surprise round of a conflict (see

result of stealth or concealment, allowing you to sneak up
on a target

act_0206.raw:
So attacks all doing

for years

act_0209.raw:

Sons, their attitude will revert to Hostile
